reasoning: Q6 shows solid progress (iteration 1 improved), currently at 29.4 ms vs DuckDB's 19.3 ms (52% gap). The evaluation identifies 5 concrete, low-to-medium risk SIMD optimizations that can cumulatively close 20-27% of the remaining gap. Prioritizing SIMD mask integration (14-20%), horizontal aggregation (6-10%), and tail vectorization (3-4%) targets the CPU-bound bottleneck with high confidence. Remaining budget allows aggressive optimization of tightly-scoped techniques.
focus_areas: Replace scalar SIMD mask extraction with direct blending/gather to eliminate store-to-load latency
Keep aggregation in SIMD registers using horizontal reduction instead of scalar accumulation
Eliminate scalar tail loop by padding arrays to SIMD boundary with sentinel values
strategy_notes: Q6 is CPU-bound with excellent fundamentals (64-thread parallelism, column pruning, correct predicate ordering, mmap I/O). The 52% gap vs DuckDB stems entirely from inefficient SIMD mask handling. Focus iteration 2 on tightening SIMD integration: (1) use _mm256_blend_pd or gather patterns to directly multiply price×discount without extracting masks to scalar arrays, (2) maintain 4 partial sums in SIMD lanes during batch loop and reduce horizontally at end, (3) pad input columns to 32-byte boundaries to eliminate ~1.5M scalar tail iterations. Combining these three optimizations should yield 20-27% improvement (6-8 ms), pushing execution toward 21-23 ms and closing ~60% of the remaining DuckDB gap. With only 1 iteration remaining after this, final polish (prefetch hints, thread count tuning) can push toward 20-21 ms if needed.
notes: Avoid algorithmic changes—code is already correct. Focus purely on SIMD instruction-level optimization. If any of these optimizations regress, fall back to prefetching and thread count tuning for iteration 3.
